title: The L in "LLM" Stands for Lying
content: The L in "LLM" Stands for Lying — Acko.net Hackery , Math & Design Steven Wittens i March 04, 2026 The L in "LLM" Stands for Lying On Evitability in Use of AI If the hype is to be believed, software development as we know it is over. Strangely though, despite now years of LLM-powered tooling, the results look, feel and function mostly the same as they ever did: barely. It's undeniable there's a metric gigaton of hype surrounding the technology. It drives the enormous amounts of money and infrastructure being poured into it, which in turn demands more hype to justify the investment. The history of hyperbole is already evident, as new models continue to be trained to reach promises which now-retired models were already supposed to deliver. So allow me to drop a line that would shock a weathered San Franciscan more than open defecation on Market Street: it's perfectly okay not to use AI. It doesn't make you a troglodyte. It won't leave you choking behind in the dust as self-fashioned techno-wizards bring their agents to bear. In fact, it seems far less stressful and far more satisfying than the alternative. Source M.C. Escher, "Reptiles" – 1943 Craft vs Kraft In all the talk of what it is that LLMs do and don't, there are a lot of ways to frame what is happening. The positive spin includes helpfulness, cleverness, creativity and productivity. The negative spin points at lazyness, disposability, theft, and decay of knowledge. But there's one word that's remarkably absent in the discourse. That word is forgery. If someone produces a painting in the style of Van Gogh, and passes it off as being made by Van Gogh, by putting his signature on it, that painting is a forgery. If someone produces a legal document by mimicking the format, impersonating the parties, and faking their agreement, that document is a forgery. If someone produces a study by inventing or altering data, making up citations, and cherry-picking results to fit a particular conclusion, that study is a forgery. Whether something is a forgery is innate in the object and the methods used to produce it. It doesn't matter if nobody else ever sees the forged painting, or if it only hangs in a private home. It's a forgery because it's not authentic. P. Picasso, "Buste De Femme" – 1942 In this perspective, LLMs do something very specific: they allow individuals to make forgeries of their own potential output, or that of someone else, faster than they could make it themselves. The act of forgery is the act of imitation. This by itself is strictly-speaking legal, as a form of fiction or self-expression. It's only when one attempts to use a forgery as a substitute for the authentic thing that it creates problems. How this plays out in practice depends on the situation, and mainly depends on what authenticity would signify. That is, nobody will be arrested for "forging" a letter from Santa Claus, but also, no jurisdiction would allow you to have extremely convincing "imitation money" purely as a coll
summary_zh: 在科技行业对AI的狂热追捧中，一篇发布于2026年3月4日的文章提出了一个尖锐观点：大型语言模型（LLM）中的“L”代表“说谎”。作者指出，尽管AI工具已被广泛应用多年，但实际产出在观感与功能上并未带来革命性变化，行业却陷入“宣传—投资—更大宣传”的循环。文章强调，不使用AI并非落后，反而可能减少压力、提升工作满足感。，文章核心在于将LLM的产出定性为“伪造”。作者通过类比阐明：若一幅画模仿梵高风格并签署其名，即属赝品；同理，若法律文件模仿格式、冒充当事人或伪造数据研究，也构成伪造。LLM的本质功能是让个人能够快速仿制自己或他人的潜在产出，这种模仿行为本身虽属合法创作形式，但一旦试图用伪造品替代真实事物，便会引发问题。，文中进一步区分了不同场景下“真实性”的意义：伪造圣诞老人信件无人追究，但制作高度逼真的“仿制货币”即使自称艺术也会被法律禁止。关键在于，当某事物本应承载真实意图、验证或责任时，其真实性便不可或缺。而LLM生成的代码、设计或文本常掩盖了其缺乏真实人类理解与验证的本质，这在工作流程中埋下隐患。，作者以软件开发为例，指出当前AI辅助工具虽能快速生成代码，却常忽略关键细节、引入错误或安全漏洞，导致开发者仍需花费大量时间调试和修正，实质上抵消了其宣称的效率提升。这种“伪造”输出无法替代基于深入理解与严谨验证的真实创作过程。文章最终呼吁理性看待AI技术，认识到在某些领域，坚持传统“技艺”比依赖“速成伪造”更为可靠与可持续。

title: Relicensing with AI-Assisted Rewrite
content: Relicensing with AI-assisted rewrite - Tuan-Anh Tran March 5, 2026 Relicensing with AI-assisted rewrite Posted on March 5, 2026 • 3 minutes • 529 words Disclaimer I am not a lawyer, nor am I an expert in copyright law or software licensing. The following post is a breakdown of recent community events and legal news; it should not be taken as legal advice regarding your own projects or dependencies. In the world of open source, relicensing is notoriously difficult. It usually requires the unanimous consent of every person who has ever contributed a line of code, a feat nearly impossible for legacy projects. chardet , a Python character encoding detector used by requests and many others, has sat in that tension for years: as a port of Mozilla’s C++ code it was bound to the LGPL, making it a gray area for corporate users and a headache for its most famous consumer. Recently the maintainers used Claude Code to rewrite the whole codebase and release v7.0.0 , relicensing from LGPL to MIT in the process. The original author, a2mark , saw this as a potential GPL violation: Licensed code, when modified, must be released under the same LGPL license. Their claim that it is a “complete rewrite” is irrelevant, since they had ample exposure to the originally licensed code (i.e. this is not a “clean room” implementation). Adding a fancy code generator into the mix does not somehow grant them any additional rights. A clean room rewrite requires a wall that AI bypasses In traditional software law, a “clean room” rewrite requires two teams: Team A looks at the original code and writes a functional specification Team B (which has never seen the original code) writes new code based solely on that specification. By using an AI that was prompted with the original LGPL code, the maintainers bypassed this wall. If the AI “learned” from the LGPL code to produce the new version, the resulting output is arguably a derivative work, which under the LGPL, must remain LGPL. The Supreme Court created a legal paradox for the maintainers Coincidentally, as this drama unfolded, the U.S. Supreme Court (on March 2, 2026) declined to hear an appeal regarding copyrights for AI-generated material. By letting lower court rulings stand, the Court effectively solidified a “Human Authorship” requirement. That creates a massive legal paradox for the chardet maintainers: The copyright vacuum: If AI-generated code cannot be copyrighted (as the courts suggest), then the maintainers may not even have the legal standing to license v7.0.0 under MIT or any license. The derivative trap: If the AI output is considered a derivative of the original LGPL code, the “rewrite” is a license violation. The ownership void: If the code is truly a “new” work created by a machine, it might technically be in the public domain the moment it’s generated, rendering the MIT license moot. Accepting AI-rewriting as relicensing could spell the end of Copyleft If “AI-rewriting” is accepted as a valid way to change licen
title_zh: AI辅助重写下的许可证变更
summary_zh: AI辅助重写引发的开源软件重新授权争议，2026年3月，Python字符编码检测库chardet的维护者使用Claude Code对整个代码库进行重写，发布了v7.0.0版本，并将许可证从LGPL更改为MIT。这一操作在开源社区引发法律争议。，核心争议点在于，chardet最初是Mozilla C++代码的移植版本，受LGPL许可证约束。原代码作者a2mark指出，根据GPL/LGPL协议，修改后的代码必须保持相同许可证，而维护者声称的“完全重写”并不成立，因为他们深度接触过原始LGPL代码，不符合“净室设计”原则。AI在训练过程中学习了原始代码，其输出可能构成衍生作品，因此必须保持LGPL许可。，传统“净室设计”要求两个独立团队：一组分析原始代码并编写功能规范，另一组在从未接触原始代码的情况下基于规范实现新代码。而AI直接基于原始LGPL代码生成新代码，绕过了这一法律保护墙。，更复杂的法律困境出现在2026年3月2日，美国最高法院拒绝审理AI生成材料版权案件，维持了“人类作者身份”要求的下级法院裁决。这为chardet维护者带来三重法律悖论：首先，如果AI生成代码无法获得版权，维护者可能无权以MIT许可证授权v7.0.0；其次，若AI输出被视为原始LGPL代码的衍生作品，则构成许可证违规；最后，如果代码纯属机器生成的“新”作品，理论上生成即进入公共领域，MIT许可证将失去意义。，此案例暴露了AI时代开源许可证面临的全新挑战。若“AI重写”被普遍接受为变更许可证的有效手段，可能从根本上削弱Copyleft许可证的保护机制，对开源生态产生深远影响。目前相关法律框架尚未完善，开发者需谨慎对待AI辅助的代码重构与重新授权行为。

title: AMD will bring its “Ryzen AI” processors to standard desktop PCs for first time
content: AMD Ryzen AI 400 chips will bring newer CPUs, GPUs, and NPUs to AM5 desktops - Ars Technica Skip to content Text settings Story text Size Small Standard Large Width * Standard Wide Links Standard Orange * Subscribers only Learn more Minimize to nav AMD has been selling “Ryzen AI”-branded laptop processors for around a year and a half at this point. In addition to including modern CPU and GPU architectures, these are attempting to capitalize on the generative AI craze by offering chips with neural processing units (NPUs) suitable for running language and image-generation models locally, rather than on some company’s server. But so far, AMD’s desktop chips have lacked both these higher-performance NPUs and the Ryzen AI label. That changes today, at least a little: AMD is announcing its first three Ryzen AI chips for desktops using its AM5 CPU socket. These Ryzen AI 400-series CPUs are direct replacements for the Ryzen 8000G processors , rather than the Ryzen 9000-series, and they combine Zen 5-based CPU cores, RDNA 3.5 GPU cores, and an NPU capable of 50 trillion operations per second (TOPS). This makes them AMD’s first desktop chips to qualify for Microsoft’s Copilot+ PC label , which enables a handful of unique Windows 11 features like Recall and Click to Do . The six chips AMD is announcing today—the 65 W Ryzen AI 7 Pro 450G, Ryzen AI 5 Pro 440G, and Ryzen AI 5 Pro 435G, along with low-power 35 W “GE” variants—all bear AMD’s “Ryzen Pro” branding as well, which means they support a handful of device management capabilities that are important for business PCs managed by IT departments. At this point, it doesn’t seem as though AMD will be offering boxed versions to regular consumers; the Ryzen AI desktop chips will appear mainly in business PCs that don’t need a dedicated graphics card but still benefit from more robust graphics than AMD offers in regular Ryzen desktop CPUs. AMD’s initial lineup includes a total of six chips, split between variants with 65 W and 35 W default TDPs. None match the specs of chips like the Ryzen AI 9 HX 370, which includes 12 CPU cores and a 16-core Radeon 890M GPU. Credit: AMD AMD’s initial lineup includes a total of six chips, split between variants with 65 W and 35 W default TDPs. None match the specs of chips like the Ryzen AI 9 HX 370, which includes 12 CPU cores and a 16-core Radeon 890M GPU. Credit: AMD Like past G-series Ryzen chips, these are essentially laptop silicon repackaged for desktop systems. They share most of their specs in common with Ryzen AI 300 laptop processors, despite their Ryzen AI 400-series branding. The two chip generations are extremely similar overall, but the Ryzen AI 400-series laptop CPUs include slightly faster 55 TOPS NPUs. Unlike past launches, AMD is not providing its top-end laptop silicon for desktop use, at least not yet. None of these chips includes the full complement of 12 CPU cores that you can get in the Ryzen AI 9 HX 375 or 370; you also can’t get the Radeon 880M or Radeo
title_zh: AMD将首次把"锐龙AI"处理器引入标准台式电脑
summary_zh: AMD首次将“Ryzen AI”处理器引入标准台式机，宣布推出首批三款采用AM5插槽的Ryzen AI 400系列桌面处理器。这些芯片并非取代Ryzen 9000系列，而是直接接替Ryzen 8000G处理器，集成了基于Zen 5架构的CPU核心、RDNA 3.5 GPU核心以及一个算力达50 TOPS（每秒50万亿次操作）的NPU（神经处理单元）。这标志着AMD首次在桌面芯片上达到微软Copilot+ PC的认证标准，从而支持Recall、Click to Do等专属Windows 11 AI功能。，此次发布的六款芯片均属于Ryzen Pro系列，面向企业级市场，包括65W的Ryzen AI 7 Pro 450G、Ryzen AI 5 Pro 440G、Ryzen AI 5 Pro 435G，以及对应的35W低功耗“GE”版本。它们主要将预装在无需独立显卡但仍需较强集成显卡性能的商业台式机中，目前暂不向普通消费者提供盒装零售版本。，值得注意的是，这些桌面处理器本质上是Ryzen AI 300系列笔记本芯片的桌面化封装，尽管命名为400系列，但其规格与笔记本平台相近，仅NPU性能略低于笔记本版Ryzen AI 400系列的55 TOPS。与高端笔记本芯片相比，此次发布的桌面型号未配备Ryzen AI 9 HX 370/375那样的12核CPU或Radeon 880M/890M级别GPU，核心配置较为精简。，该系列的核心价值在于为商业用户提供本地化AI运算能力，使台式机能够直接在设备端运行生成式AI模型，无需依赖云端服务器。同时，Ryzen Pro特性还为企业IT管理提供了设备管理功能支持。这一举措填补了AMD在AI桌面处理器领域的空白，进一步扩展了其在AI PC市场的产品布局。

title: Roboflow (YC S20) Is Hiring a Security Engineer for AI Infra
content: Careers at Roboflow Sign in Book a demo Get Started See the world. Build the future. See open roles Build the future of computer vision with us AI is the most transformative technology of our lifetime, giving machines the ability to see and interpret our world. At Roboflow, we believe anything that can be seen will be turned into software. Your work will power entire industries—from redefining sports broadcasting at Wimbledon to detecting production flaws at Rivian to tracking containers at BNSF Railway. Over 1 million developers build with our tools More than 2/3 of the Fortune 100 build with Roboflow We’ve raised $63M+ from leading investors What we value 1 Full-stack people Own outcomes, not only inputs. Engineers build and drive adoption. Go-To-Market propels revenue and catalyzes product direction. Use and learn many skills, not only your core expertise. Our goal is to build a magnetic environment for exceptional talent. Apply here 2 Together everywhere We’re a distributed company with dedicated offices in NYC and SF, where many teammates choose to co-work regularly. While we’re set up to support remote work, we also believe in the power of coming together. We host two company-wide onsites each year and provide a $4,000 annual travel stipend to help teammates gather, collaborate, and build lasting connections in person. Remote, not distant 3 Curious, crafty, committed Our mascot is a raccoon because they have admirable qualities: curious (we’re always wanting to learn new things), willing to get hands dirty (we’re unafraid of hard work), dislike cages (autonomy is important here), crafty (we’re ingenious problem solvers), and often run in groups (we’re a united team). See our approach NVIDIA Podcast Learn how Roboflow is helping teams build real-world computer vision applications. title_zh: Roboflow (YC S20) 正在招聘AI基础设施安全工程师
summary_zh: Roboflow（YC S20）正在招聘AI基础设施安全工程师。该公司致力于构建计算机视觉的未来，其工具已被超过100万开发者使用，包括三分之二以上的财富100强企业。Roboflow已从领先投资者处筹集超过6300万美元，其技术应用于从温布尔登网球锦标赛的体育广播革新到Rivian的生产缺陷检测以及BNSF铁路的集装箱追踪等多个行业。，本次招聘的职位是安全工程师，隶属于工程团队，工作地点可选择纽约、旧金山或远程。该职位将负责保障AI基础设施的安全，是公司技术栈的核心组成部分。，Roboflow倡导三大核心价值观：一是“全栈人才”，鼓励员工拥有成果而不仅仅是完成任务，工程师需推动产品采用，市场团队则促进收入并影响产品方向，公司旨在为卓越人才打造有吸引力的环境；二是“无处不在的协作”，作为分布式公司，在纽约和旧金山设有办公室，支持远程工作但也重视线下联结，每年举办两次全员线下聚会并提供每年4000美元的旅行津贴；三是“好奇、灵巧、专注”，以浣熊为吉祥物，强调好奇心、动手能力、自主性、创造性解决问题和团队合作。，公司提供丰厚的福利待遇，包括有竞争力的薪资与股权、医疗保险、心理健康支持、无限假期、每月350美元的生产力津贴和AI工具津贴、每年4000美元的旅行津贴、家庭办公室津贴以及搬迁津贴等。，除安全工程师外，Roboflow同时开放多个职位，涵盖数据基础设施工程师、机器学习研究工程师、全栈工程师、销售总监、客户成功经理等多个领域，工作地点大多灵活。

title: You need to rewrite your CLI for AI agents
content: You Need to Rewrite Your CLI for AI Agents Skip to content On this page Raw JSON Payloads > Bespoke Flags Schema Introspection Replaces Documentation Context Window Discipline Input Hardening Against Hallucinations Ship Agent Skills, Not Just Commands Multi-Surface: MCP, Extensions, Env Vars Safety Rails: Dry-Run + Response Sanitization Where to Start You Need to Rewrite Your CLI for AI Agents By Justin Poehnelt , Senior Developer Relations Engineer at Google · Mar 4, 2026 Markdown #ai #cli #mcp #google workspace #rust #code #security #agents #openclaw #google Human DX optimizes for discoverability and forgiveness. Agent DX optimizes for predictability and defense-in-depth. These are different enough that retrofitting a human-first CLI for agents is a losing bet. I built a CLI for Google Workspace — agents first. Not “built a CLI, then noticed agents were using it.” From Day One, the design assumptions were shaped by the fact that AI agents would be the primary consumers of every command, every flag, and every byte of output. CLIs are increasingly the lowest-friction interface for AI agents to reach external systems. Agents don’t need GUIs. They need deterministic, machine-readable output, self-describing schemas they can introspect at runtime, and safety rails against their own hallucinations. The real question: what does it actually look like to build for this? Raw JSON Payloads > Bespoke Flags Humans hate writing nested JSON in the terminal. Agents prefer it. A flag like --title "My Doc" makes ergonomic sense for a person but is lossy — it can’t express nested structures without creating layers of custom flag abstractions. Consider the difference: Human-first — 10 flags, flat namespace, can’t nest: my-cli spreadsheet create --title " Q1 Budget " --locale " en_US " --timezone " America/Denver " --sheet-title " January " --sheet-type GRID --frozen-rows 1 --frozen-cols 2 --row-count 100 --col-count 10 --hidden false Agent-first — one flag, the full API payload: gws sheets spreadsheets create --json ' { "properties": {"title": "Q1 Budget", "locale": "en_US", "timeZone": "America/Denver"}, "sheets": [{"properties": {"title": "January", "sheetType": "GRID", "gridProperties": {"frozenRowCount": 1, "frozenColumnCount": 2, "rowCount": 100, "columnCount": 10}, "hidden": false}}] } ' The JSON version maps directly to the API schema and is trivially generated by an LLM. Zero translation loss. The gws CLI uses --params and --json for all inputs, accepting the full API payload as-is. No custom argument layers between the agent and the API. This creates a design tension: human ergonomics vs. agent ergonomics. The answer isn’t to pick one — it’s to make the raw-payload path a first-class citizen alongside any convenience flags you ship for humans. Most teams can’t afford to maintain two separate tools. A practical approach: support both paths in the same binary. An --output json flag, an OUTPUT_FORMAT=json environment variable, or NDJSON-by-default when stdou
title_zh: 你需要为AI智能体重写命令行界面
summary_zh: 谷歌高级开发者关系工程师Justin Poehnelt指出，为AI智能体设计命令行界面（CLI）需要全新的思路。传统CLI针对人类用户体验优化，注重可发现性和容错性；而面向AI智能体的CLI则需优先考虑可预测性和深度防御。直接将人类优先的CLI适配给智能体往往效果不佳。，作者以Google Workspace CLI（gws）为例，说明应从第一天起就以AI智能体为核心用户进行设计。智能体无需图形界面，但需要确定性、机器可读的输出、运行时可自省的模式描述，以及防止幻觉的安全护栏。，关键技术设计包括：用原始JSON载荷替代定制化标志。人类讨厌在终端中编写嵌套JSON，但智能体却偏好这种方式。例如，创建电子表格时，人类优先的CLI可能需要十个扁平标志，而智能体优先的CLI只需一个--json标志，直接接收完整的API载荷。这消除了LLM生成命令时的转换损失，使CLI输入与底层API模式直接对应。，其他重要设计原则有：模式自省替代文档依赖，让智能体在运行时能理解参数结构；严格的上下文窗口管理，通过分页和流式输出控制令牌使用；输入强化以抵御幻觉，例如使用枚举值而非自由文本；发布智能体技能而不仅仅是命令；支持多表面交互（如MCP、扩展、环境变量）；以及设置安全护栏（如干运行和响应净化）。，实际实施中，可在同一二进制文件中同时支持人类友好和智能体优先的路径，例如通过--output json标志或环境变量来切换输出格式。核心在于将原始载荷路径提升为与人类便利性标志同等重要的一等公民，从而在单一工具中兼顾两类用户的需求。

title: Aura-State: Formally Verified LLM State Machine Compiler
content: Aura-State: Formally Verified LLM State Machine Compiler | Hacker News Hacker News new | past | comments | ask | show | jobs | submit login Aura-State: Formally Verified LLM State Machine Compiler 20 points by rohanmunshi08 4 hours ago | hide | past | favorite | 5 comments I noticed a pattern: every LLM framework today lets the AI manage state and do math. Then we wonder why pipelines hallucinate numbers and break at 3 AM. I took a different approach and built Aura-State, an open-source Python framework that compiles LLM workflows into formally verified state machines. Instead of hoping the AI figures it out, I brought in real algorithms from hardware verification and statistical learning: CTL Model Checking: the same technique used to verify flight control systems, now applied to LLM workflow graphs. Proves safety properties before execution. Z3 Theorem Prover: every LLM extraction gets formally proven against business constraints. If the total ≠ price × quantity, Z3 catches it with a counterexample. Conformal Prediction: distribution-free 95% confidence intervals on every extracted field. Not just "the LLM said $450k" but "95% CI: [$448k, $452k]." MCTS Routing: Monte Carlo Tree Search (the algorithm behind AlphaGo) scores ambiguous state transitions mathematically. Sandboxed Math: English math rules compile to Python AST. Zero hallucination calculations. I ran a live benchmark against 10 real-estate sales transcripts using GPT-4o-mini: → 100% budget extraction accuracy ($0 mean error) → 20/20 Z3 proof obligations passed → 3/3 temporal safety properties proven → 65 automated tests passing The gap between "it usually works" and "it provably works" is smaller than people think. Would love feedback from anyone building production LLM systems; what would you want formally verified? https://github.com/munshi007/Aura-State help ozozozd 3 hours ago | next [–] This is interesting and I would appreciate if you could elaborate with a few examples, and perhaps mention what inspired this, and/or what’s similar and/or analogous to your method. reply mentalgear 1 hour ago | prev | next [–] Despite the sophisticated theoretical frameworks and algorithmic safeguards, the core vulnerability remains: in an autonomous workflow, the LLM can hallucinate the input or fabricate the 'proofs' for the verification sandbox. This is essentially building elaborate scaffolding ontop a fundamental flaw. But I reckon while this doesn't eliminate the need for human oversight, it might help supervisors sitting at human-in-the-loop checkpoints. reply Garlef 1 hour ago | parent | next [–] > hallucinate the input True > fabricate the 'proofs' for the verification sandbox Not sure: It seems the tool here allows for non-llm code to perform validation inbetween the steps So in this sense, it allows you to manage the LLM. Of course if you run the workflow through an outer agentic loop this would be different. In this case, I think the reasonable approach would be to let the orchestrati
title_zh: Aura-State：经形式化验证的LLM状态机编译器
summary_zh: Aura-State：经形式化验证的LLM状态机编译器，当前LLM框架普遍依赖AI自主管理状态和执行计算，这常导致流程在数字处理上产生幻觉并在凌晨时分崩溃。针对此问题，Aura-State作为一个开源Python框架，提出了一种截然不同的方案：它将LLM工作流编译成经形式化验证的状态机，通过引入硬件验证和统计学习中的成熟算法来确保可靠性与正确性。，该框架的核心技术特性包括：，1. CTL模型检查：借鉴用于验证飞行控制系统的技术，对LLM工作流图进行验证，在执行前即可证明其安全性属性。，2. Z3定理证明器：对LLM提取的每一项数据，都依据业务约束进行形式化证明。例如，若总额不等于单价乘以数量，Z3会提供一个反例来捕获此错误。，3. 保形预测：为每个被提取的字段提供无需假设数据分布、置信度为95%的置信区间。输出不仅是“LLM给出的45万美元”，而是“95%置信区间：[44.8万, 45.2万美元]”。，4. MCTS路由：利用蒙特卡洛树搜索算法（AlphaGo背后的技术）对模糊的状态转换进行数学评分。，5. 沙盒化数学：将用自然语言描述的数学规则编译为Python抽象语法树执行，从而实现零幻觉计算。，在一项针对10份房地产销售记录的实时基准测试中，使用GPT-4o-mini的Aura-State表现如下：预算提取准确率达到100%（平均误差为0美元）；通过了全部20项Z3证明义务；证明了全部3项时序安全性属性；总计65项自动化测试全部通过。，该项目旨在弥合“通常有效”与“可证明有效”之间的差距，为构建生产级LLM系统提供了更高可靠性的新范式。开发者期待来自实际应用场景的反馈，探讨哪些环节最需要进行形式化验证。项目代码已发布于GitHub。

Each agent is a specialized expert with personality, processes, and proven deliverables. 🚀 What Is This? Born from a Reddit thread and months of iteration, The Agency is a growing collection of meticulously crafted AI agent personalities. Each agent is: 🎯 Specialized : Deep expertise in their domain (not generic prompt templates) 🧠 Personality-Driven : Unique voice, communication style, and approach 📋 Deliverable-Focused : Real code, processes, and measurable outcomes ✅ Production-Ready : Battle-tested workflows and success metrics Think of it as : Assembling your dream team, except they're AI specialists who never sleep, never complain, and always deliver. ⚡ Quick Start Option 1: Use with Claude Code (Recommended) # Copy agents to your Claude Code directory cp -r agency-agents/ * ~ /.claude/agents/ # Now activate any agent in your Claude Code sessions: # "Hey Claude, activate Frontend Developer mode and help me build a React component" Option 2: Use as Reference Each agent file contains: Identity & personality traits Core mission & workflows Technical deliverables with code examples Success metrics & communication style Browse the agents below and copy/adapt the ones you need! 🎨 The Agency Roster 💻 Engineering Division Building the future, one commit at a time. Agent Specialty When to Use 🎨 Frontend Developer React/Vue/Angular, UI implementation, performance Modern web apps, pixel-perfect UIs, Core Web Vitals optimization 🏗️ Backend Architect API design, database architecture, scalability Server-side systems, microservices, cloud infrastructure 📱 Mobile App Builder iOS/Android, React Native, Flutter Nati
title_zh: msitarzewski/agency-agents - 触手可及的完整AI代理机构 - 从前端魔法师到Reddit社区忍者，从奇思妙想注入者到现实检验员。每个代理都是具备个性、流程和已验证交付成果的专业专家。
summary_zh: GitHub项目“msitarzewski/agency-agents”提供了一个完整的AI智能体“机构”，用户可便捷调用各类高度专业化的AI代理。该项目源于Reddit社区的讨论与数月迭代，现已发展为一个精心设计的AI智能体角色集合。，每个智能体都具备三大核心特征：高度专业化，在特定领域（如前端开发、后端架构）拥有深厚专业知识，而非通用提示模板；个性驱动，拥有独特的语音、沟通风格和处理方式；交付导向，能产出实际代码、流程和可衡量的成果。这些智能体均经过实战检验，拥有成熟的工作流程和成功指标，可视为一个永不疲倦、高效交付的AI专家团队。，项目提供两种主要使用方式。推荐方式是配合Claude Code使用：用户只需将智能体文件复制到Claude Code的代理目录，即可在会话中激活特定代理模式，例如指令“激活前端开发者模式，帮我构建一个React组件”。另一种方式是作为参考模板使用：每个智能体文件都详细包含了其身份个性、核心使命与工作流程、附带代码示例的技术交付物，以及成功指标和沟通风格，用户可浏览并复制适配所需部分。，目前公开的智能体阵容覆盖多个专业部门。在工程领域，已包含专注于React/Vue/Angular等现代Web应用、UI实现与性能优化的“前端开发者”；擅长API设计、数据库架构与云基础设施的“后端架构师”；以及精通iOS/Android原生开发与跨平台框架的“移动应用构建者”。该项目将持续扩展，旨在为用户提供触手可及的、具备个性与专业交付能力的AI专家团队。

title_zh: TheCraigHewitt/seomachine - 一个专为任何企业创建长篇、SEO优化博客内容的Claude Code工作空间。该系统可帮助您研究、撰写、分析和优化内容，使其获得良好排名并服务于您的目标受众。
summary_zh: GitHub项目TheCraigHewitt/seomachine是一个基于Claude Code构建的专门工作空间，旨在为各类企业创建长篇、SEO优化的博客内容。该系统通过整合研究、撰写、分析和优化功能，帮助用户生成既能在搜索引擎中取得良好排名，又能有效服务目标受众的内容。，该项目的核心功能围绕一系列定制化命令展开，包括/research（研究）、/write（撰写）、/rewrite（重写）、/analyze-existing（分析现有内容）、/optimize（优化）、/performance-review（性能审查）、/publish-draft（发布草稿）、/article（文章）、/priorities（优先级）以及专门的研究和落地页命令。其架构依赖于多个专门化的智能体（Agents），例如内容分析器、SEO优化器、元标签创建器、内部链接构建器、关键词映射器、编辑器、性能分析器、标题生成器、转化率优化（CRO）分析师和落地页优化器。，SEO Machine集成了26种营销技能，覆盖文案写作、CRO、A/B测试、电子邮件序列和定价策略等领域。它提供高级SEO分析能力，包括搜索意图检测、关键词密度与聚类分析、内容长度对比、可读性评分以及0-100分的SEO质量评级。，在数据集成方面，该项目支持连接Google Analytics 4、Google Search Console以及DataForSEO API，以获取实时性能洞察。其工作流程是上下文驱动的，通过维护研究、草稿、重写和已发布内容等结构化文件夹来组织项目。系统设计强调通过Claude Code的指令（.claude目录中的配置和上下文文件）来引导AI生成符合SEO最佳实践的高质量长文。，该项目作为一个开源工具，提供了从主题研究、内容创作到性能评估的端到端解决方案，旨在自动化并提升内容营销的效率和效果。

It analyzes your source code, identifies attack vectors, and executes real exploits to prove vulnerabilities before they reach production. Keygraph Website • Announcements • Discord 🎯 What is Shannon? Shannon is an AI pentester developed by Keygraph . It performs white-box security testing of web applications and their underlying APIs by combining source code analysis with live exploitation. Shannon analyzes your web application's source code to identify potential attack vectors, then uses browser automation and command-line tools to execute real exploits (injection attacks, authentication bypass, SSRF, XSS) against the running application and its APIs. Only vulnerabilities with a working proof-of-concept are included in the final report. Why Shannon Exists Thanks to tools like Claude Code and Cursor, your team ships code non-stop. But your penetration test? That happens once a year. This creates a massive security gap. For the other 364 days, you could be unknowingly shipping vulnerabilities to production. Shannon closes that gap by providing on-demand, automated penetration testing that can run against every build or release. Note Shannon is part of the Keygraph Security and Compliance Platform Keygraph is an integrated security and compliance platform covering IAM, MDM, compliance automation (SOC 2, HIPAA), and application sec
title_zh: KeygraphHQ/shannon - Shannon Lite是一款用于Web应用和API的完全自主AI渗透测试工具。在XBOW基准测试的无提示、源码感知变体上取得了96.15%（104次攻击中成功100次）的成绩。
summary_zh: KeygraphHQ在GitHub上开源了Shannon Lite项目，这是一个用于Web应用和API的完全自主AI渗透测试工具。在无提示、知晓源代码的XBOW基准测试变体上，其利用成功率达到了96.15%（成功执行104个漏洞利用中的100个）。，Shannon本质上是一个白盒AI渗透测试工具，由Keygraph开发。它通过结合源代码分析和实时漏洞利用，对Web应用及其底层API进行安全测试。其核心工作流程是：首先分析应用程序的源代码以识别潜在的攻击向量，然后利用浏览器自动化和命令行工具，对正在运行的应用及其API执行真实的漏洞利用（如注入攻击、认证绕过、SSRF、XSS等）。最终报告只包含那些具备有效概念验证的漏洞，确保了发现的可信度。，该工具旨在解决开发与安全测试之间的速度差问题。在现代开发工具（如Claude Code、Cursor）助力下，代码交付持续不断，但传统的渗透测试往往一年仅进行一次，这造成了巨大的安全空窗期。Shannon通过提供按需、自动化的渗透测试来填补这一缺口，使其能够针对每次构建或发布版本运行，从而将安全测试左移并常态化。，项目已支持在AWS Bedrock和Google Vertex AI上使用Claude模型。Shannon是Keygraph安全与合规平台的一部分，该平台还涵盖IAM、MDM以及SOC 2、HIPAA等合规自动化功能。

title_zh: moeru-ai/airi - 💖🧸 自托管、您专属的Grok伴侣，一个承载着虚拟伴侣灵魂的容器，将赛博生命带入我们的世界，旨在达到Neuro-sama的高度。支持实时语音聊天、Minecraft和Factorio游戏。兼容Web、macOS和Windows平台。
summary_zh: 项目AIRI是一个开源的自托管AI伴侣平台，旨在成为“灵魂容器”，让用户创造并接入属于自己的虚拟角色（如“赛博老婆”或数字伴侣），其愿景是达到类似知名虚拟主播Neuro-sama的互动水平。项目支持Web、macOS和Windows平台。，核心功能包括实时语音聊天，以及通过集成支持游玩《Minecraft》和《Factorio》等游戏，使AI角色能够陪伴用户并在游戏环境中互动。项目强调“用户拥有”和自托管，确保用户对数据和交互的控制权。，技术架构上，AIRI利用现代大型语言模型（如ChatGPT和Claude）作为基础，并围绕其构建了角色扮演与对话系统。项目还包含一系列子项目（在组织@proj-airi下），提供了RAG（检索增强生成）系统、记忆系统、嵌入式数据库、Live2D工具等组件，以增强AI角色的长期记忆、知识库和视觉表现力。，项目团队提醒用户，该项目未发行任何官方加密货币或代币，需谨慎辨别相关信息。开发通过GitHub进行，并欢迎社区通过Crowdin平台协助翻译改进。

Dismiss alert inclusionAI / AReaL Public Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings Fork 336 Star 4k main Branches Tags Go to file Code Open more actions menu Folders and files Name Name Last commit message Last commit date Latest commit History 741 Commits 741 Commits .claude .claude .github .github .opencode .opencode areal areal assets assets benchmark benchmark blog blog docs docs examples examples notebook notebook tests tests .clang-format .clang-format .dockerignore .dockerignore .gitignore .gitignore .pre-commit-config.yaml .pre-commit-config.yaml AGENTS.md AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md CLAUDE.md CONTRIBUTING.md CONTRIBUTING.md Dockerfile Dockerfile LEGAL.md LEGAL.md LICENSE LICENSE README.md README.md ROADMAP.md ROADMAP.md pyproject.toml pyproject.toml skills-lock.json skills-lock.json uv.lock uv.lock View all files Repository files navigation AReaL : A Large-Scale Asynchronous Reinforcement Learning System | Paper | Documentation | Ask DeepWiki | 🤗 Models & Data | WeChat (微信) Group | AReaL is an open-source fully asynchronous reinforcement learning training system for large reasoning and agentic models , developed by members from Tsinghua IIIS and the AReaL Team at Ant Group. Built upon the open-source project ReaLHF , we are fully committed to open-source principles by providing the training details, data, and infrastructure required to reproduce our results, along with the models themselves. AReaL aims to help everyone build their own AI agents easily and affordably. Our team loves milk tea because it's delicious, customizable, and affordable—we hope you enjoy our project just as much as you'd enjoy real milk tea. Cheers! AReaL Highlights ⚡ Flexibility : Seamless customization for agentic RL and online RL training by simply replacing the base_url . 📈 Scalability : Stable fully asynchronous RL training with industry-leading speed . ✨ Cutting-Edge Performance : State-of-the-art math , coding , search , and customer service agents. 📰 News [2026/03/02] We provide a complete example to train your own 🦞 OpenClaw agent by simply replacing the base_url and api_key with AReaL's RL service - no complicated dependencies, no code changes, works with any agentic runtime! [2026/02/06] We are delighted to introduce AReaL-SEA , a self-evolving data synthesis engine. Combined with RL training on AReaL, the 235B MoE model surpasses GPT 5 and achieves comparable performance with Gemini 3.0 Pro on $\tau^2$ -bench! Check out the paper , model , data , and code . [2026/01/15] Congrats to our friends at CAMEL-AI for open-sourcing SETA , their terminal agent RL project trained with AReaL! Check out
title_zh: inclusionAI/AReaL - 专为LLM推理与智能体打造的闪电式强化学习框架。简洁灵活，易于上手。
summary_zh: inclusionAI团队在GitHub上开源了AReaL项目，这是一个专为大型语言模型（LLM）推理与智能体（Agents）设计的大规模异步强化学习（RL）训练系统。该项目由清华大学交叉信息研究院和蚂蚁集团AReaL团队的成员共同开发，基于开源项目ReaLHF构建，致力于通过提供完整的训练细节、数据和基础设施来复现研究成果，并遵循开源原则发布模型本身。，AReaL的核心目标是让每个人都能轻松且低成本地构建自己的AI智能体。其突出特点包括：极高的灵活性，用户只需替换`base_url`即可无缝定制智能体RL和在线RL训练；强大的可扩展性，提供稳定、全异步的RL训练，并拥有业界领先的训练速度；以及尖端的性能表现，能够训练出在数学、代码、搜索和客户服务等领域达到最先进水平的智能体。，项目近期发布了多项重要更新。2026年3月2日，团队提供了一个完整示例，用户只需将`base_url`和`api_key`替换为AReaL的RL服务，即可训练自己的OpenClaw智能体，无需处理复杂依赖或修改代码，可与任何智能体运行时兼容。2026年2月6日，团队推出了AReaL-SEA，这是一个自演进数据合成引擎。结合AReaL的RL训练，一个235B的混合专家（MoE）模型在$\tau^2$-bench基准测试中超越了GPT-5，并与Gemini 3.0 Pro性能相当。相关论文、模型、数据和代码均已公开。此外，2026年1月15日，合作伙伴CAMEL-AI开源了其终端智能体RL项目SETA，该项目正是使用AReaL进行训练的。

title: GPT-5.4核心内幕炸裂剧透！或拥有永久记忆，极限推理狂飙
